title: fix: output pollen costs in fixed-point notation
author: pollinations-ai[bot]

gist.headline: No more math homework
gist.blurb: We fixed a bug where API model costs were showing up in scientific notation. Now you get clean, readable decimals instead of trying to parse 1.65e-7.
gist.summary: Updated the models API endpoints to return pollen costs as fixed-point decimal strings rather than scientific notation.
gist.impact: Developers querying model pricing will now receive easily readable decimal values instead of scientific notation, simplifying cost parsing.
gist.visual_concept: The Nomnom creature eating complex math equations or scientific notation (like '1.65e-7') while the bee mascot holds a clean, simple decimal number ('0.000000165').
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round nomnom creature happily eating a glowing scientific notation math equation '1.65e-7' while the cute bee mascot holds up a clean sign showing '0.000000165'. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like A Short Hike. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
